spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: navigator.storage.estimate() returns identical quota in normal and incognito. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Live interactive controls present and wired up — passes the no-static-card rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is well covered; edge cases / failure modes from the spec (see open questions) are not exercised on this concept page.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section as the design system requires.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Allocate measurable storage and show usage move while quota holds
openQuestions.detail: Right now estimate.usage reports near-zero. Writing a few MB into a sandbox via Cache API and re-reading would show the formula 'max(10 GiB, ceil(actual)) + usage' in action with usage drifting upward.

summary: One-button estimate() readout with bucketed-formula explanation. Main gap: Allocate measurable storage and show usage move while quota holds.
